# Epic 1 Retrospective — Project Scaffolding & First Auto-Generated View

- **Date:** 2026-04-27
- **Facilitator:** Amelia (Developer)
- **Project Lead:** Jerome
- **Participants:** Amelia (Developer), Alice (Product Owner), Charlie (Senior Dev), Dana (QA Engineer), Elena (Junior Dev), Winston (Architect), Paige (Tech Writer), Murat (Test Architect)
- **Status:** First retrospective for the project — no prior retro to reference.

---

## 1. Epic Summary

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Stories completed | 8 / 8 |
| Story IDs | 1-1 Solution Structure & Build Infrastructure · 1-2 Contracts Package · 1-3 Fluxor State Management Foundation · 1-4 Source Generator Parse Stage · 1-5 Source Generator Transform & Emit Stages · 1-6 Counter Sample Domain & Aspire Topology · 1-7 CI Pipeline & Semantic Release · 1-8 Hot Reload & Fluent UI Contingency |
| Review rounds | 1-5: 2 rounds · 1-8: 3 rounds · all others: 1 round |
| Build state at retro | Contracts 91/0/0, Shell 1184/0/3 (3 pre-existing E2E skips), SourceTools 486/0/0, Bench 2/0/0 |
| Foundational ADRs ratified | ADR-001..008 (deps pattern, Fluent UI v5 RC pin, 3-stage pipeline, project structure, Fluxor shape) |

Scope shipped: solution scaffold → Contracts package → Fluxor foundation → source-generator Parse/Transform/Emit pipeline → Counter sample on Aspire → CI + semantic-release → hot reload + Fluent UI v5 contingency plan.

---

## 2. What Went Well

1. **"Mirror EventStore" CI strategy (Story 1-7)** — copying the EventStore CI/CD with four documented divergences (`submodules: recursive`, `DiffEngine_Disabled`, advisory mode, solution-level testing) saved enormous design time. Reusable pattern for any future CI work.
2. **Diagnostics-as-data in the source generator (Story 1-4)** — `ParseResult(DomainModel?, EquatableArray<DiagnosticInfo>)` lets pure transforms run outside `SourceProductionContext`. The pattern propagated cleanly into Stories 1-5 and beyond and is still in active use through Epic 5.
3. **`IState<T>` + `IDisposable` subscription discipline (Story 1-3)** — refusing `FluxorComponent` upfront kept AOT viable and preserved a migration path off Fluxor without touching component code.
4. **Two-file deps pattern (Story 1-1)** — `deps.local.props` / `deps.nuget.props` with one `UseNuGetDeps` switch killed scattered MSBuild conditionals before they could spread.
5. **Hard package-boundary discipline (Story 1-2)** — keeping `IProjectionRenderer` *out* of Contracts because it returns `RenderFragment` (Blazor dep). The dependency-free Contracts package has held its line through Epic 5.
6. **Verify-snapshot strategy stability** — the Verify `.verified.txt` snapshot format and AngleSharp DOM-comparison strategy adopted in Story 1-5 survived 8 stories without a format change.

---

## 3. Challenges & Recurring Pain

| Pattern | Where it hit | Root cause |
|---|---|---|
| Architecture-doc drift | 1-1 (xUnit v2→v3), 1-2 (ValueTask→Task, IProjectionRenderer placement), 1-5 (hint-name pattern) | Architecture doc authored before submodule alignment was verified. Each story carried a "Version Discrepancy Alert" section. |
| `netstandard2.0` constraint list rediscovered each story | 1-2, 1-4, 1-5 each re-list constraints (no `ValueTask`, no `required`, no DIM, no `Span<T>`, `IsExternalInit` polyfill) | No central cheat sheet — each story's dev notes re-derive it. |
| MSBuild walk-up shadowing | 1-1 (FrontComposerRoot guard), 1-7 (`src/Directory.Build.props` must `<Import>` parent or it shadows root config) | Walk-up stops at first match. Easy to forget when adding subdirectory props files. |
| Tasks checked off while sub-criteria deferred | 1-8 (Task 1 `[x]` with HUMAN-EXECUTED steps TODO; subtask 2.3 `[x]` with contract (a) explicitly deferred) | Definition-of-Done for "task complete" wasn't tight enough — partial completion got the box. |
| Late-story review thrash | 1-5 (2 rounds), 1-8 (3 rounds) | Story specs reserved diagnostic IDs (HFC1001, HFC1010) and asserted features (BoundedContext DisplayLabel) not actually wired end-to-end. First reviewer caught the gap; later rounds caught the patches' patches. |
| Verify diff-tool hangs CI | 1-7 — required `DiffEngine_Disabled: true` env var | Defaults of dev-only tools leaked into CI when adopted at scale (16+ snapshots). |
| Tests passing without verifying behavior | 1-3, 1-4 reviews | AC-mapped tests asserted on shape, not on the AC's actual claim. |

---

## 4. Key Lessons

1. **Architecture-doc reconciliation is a story-1.x recurring tax.** Every Epic 1 story rediscovered architecture/reality drift. Going forward, the doc should be updated *as a result of* the story, not before it.
2. **Definition of Done must require zero deferred sub-items checked off.** Story 1-8 Round 2 surfaced this; the rule needs to be a habit. Aligns with existing memory feedback `feedback_no_manual_validation.md` (prefer automated validation over `[HUMAN-EXECUTED]` manual steps).
3. **Late-story review thrash drops dramatically when AC tests assert *behavior*, not *shape*.** Stories 1-3 and 1-4 reviews demonstrate this — the patch was always "this test passes without verifying the claim."
4. **Pure-function pipelines + diagnostics-as-data is the right shape for source generators.** `ParseResult.Diagnostics` carrying `EquatableArray<DiagnosticInfo>` worked across 8 stories; no rework needed.
5. **Cross-cutting constraints (`netstandard2.0`, MSBuild walk-up) belong in one cheat sheet, not 8.** Each rediscovery is wasted budget.

---

## 5. Action Items

| # | Action | Owner | When | Why |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Extract `netstandard2.0` + MSBuild walk-up cheat sheet to `docs/cross-cutting-constraints.md`; reference from every SourceTools/Contracts story instead of re-deriving | Paige (Tech Writer) | Before next SourceTools story | Repeated 3× across Epic 1; pattern continues into Epic 2/4. |
| 2 | Update `architecture.md` to match Epic 1+2 reality (xUnit v3, Task-not-ValueTask, `IProjectionRenderer` in Shell not Contracts, `{Namespace}.{TypeName}` hint pattern, generated-file output paths) | Winston (Architect) | Before Epic 6 (customization gradient depends on accurate ADRs) | Five stories carry "Version Discrepancy Alert" sections; doc is now authoritative-on-paper, divergent-in-code. |
| 3 | Tighten Definition-of-Done rule: "A task can only be checked when *all sub-criteria are completed or explicitly transferred to a future-story owner with link*" — codify as story-creation-lessons L12 | Amelia (Developer) | Apply on next story | Story 1-8 Round 2 finding generalizes; Jerome's `feedback_no_manual_validation.md` already captures the spirit. |
| 4 | Add AC-test review checkpoint: "Does this test FAIL if the AC's behavior is removed?" as Round-1 reviewer rubric | Murat (Test Architect) | Apply on next code-review run | Stories 1-3 and 1-4 reviews caught shape-only tests; rubric makes it a habit. |
| 5 | Mark `epic-1: done` in `sprint-status.yaml` (currently still `in-progress` despite 8/8 stories done) | This retrospective | Now | Status hygiene — completed alongside this retro. |
| 6 | Decide whether to run Epic 2/3/4 retrospectives (also `optional` and overdue) or roll the lessons forward into a single condensed multi-epic retro | Jerome | Confirm direction post-retro | Three more retros are pending; capacity vs. value tradeoff. |

## 6. Technical Debt Carried Forward

Already tracked in `deferred-work.md` — flagging here for Epic 6 / Epic 9 owners:

- **DisplayLabel conflicts across `[BoundedContext]`** (1-5 R2) → Epic 6 customization gradient
- **HFC1010 analyzer implementation** (1-8) → Epic 9 hot-reload pass
- **Fluent UI prerelease NU5104 packing failure** (1-7) → resolves when Fluent UI v5 GA ships
- **`[BoundedContext]` not allowed on `Struct`** (1-2 contract scope) → Epic 6
- **Namespace-collision-safe source naming for exotic namespaces** (1-5 R2) → Epic 6/9
- **Submodule gitlinks recording `-dirty`** (1-8 R3) → repo hygiene, ongoing

---

## 7. Significant Discoveries Affecting Future Epics

**Epic 6 (Developer Customization Gradient) seam tightening required.**

The customization gradient depends on `[BoundedContext]` being a stable, well-understood seam. Epic 1 left that seam with cracks: no struct support, no DisplayLabel conflict diagnostic, namespace-collision-safe source naming still pending. When Epic 6 stories 6-1 through 6-3 are created, the `[BoundedContext]` seam tightening should be a named binding decision in Story 6-1's cross-story contract table — not absorbed implicitly.

**Epic update required:** No (Epic 6 not yet broken into stories; prevention rather than correction).

---

## 8. Readiness Assessment

| Dimension | Status |
|---|---|
| Story-level completeness | 8/8 done, all reviews closed (1-5 R2 done, 1-8 R3 done) |
| Build & test | Green — Contracts 91/0/0, Shell 1184/0/3, SourceTools 486/0/0, Bench 2/0/0 |
| Deployment | Counter sample runs in Aspire topology end-to-end |
| Stakeholder acceptance | Implicit — Epic 2–5 already built on Epic 1 foundation without rollback |
| Technical health | Solid — patterns established in Epic 1 held through Epic 5 |
| Unresolved blockers | None blocking; tracked debt in `deferred-work.md` |

**Verdict:** Epic 1 is fully complete. Status flipped to `done`. No blockers carried into already-in-progress epics.

---

## 9. Closing

This was the first retrospective for the project, so it doubles as the baseline for retro practice. The patterns it established — ADRs ratified, source-generator pipeline shape, Fluxor subscription discipline, deps-switch convention, package boundary lines — held through all subsequent epics that have shipped (2, 3, 4, partially 5). That is the strongest signal Epic 1 succeeded: nothing had to be undone.

The action items above target the few recurring frictions (architecture-doc drift, `netstandard2.0` cheat-sheet repetition, premature task-checkbox-marking, shape-only AC tests) so they don't repeat in Epics 6+.
